positive: If I make them feel guilty enough for forgetting our anniversary, they will be too desperate to please me to bring up their own complaints.

positive: I'll let them believe they've won a major concession here, while I quietly keep the part that actually matters.

positive: I can see they are getting nervous, so I'll stay silent for a while and let the tension force their hand.

positive: I will frame this as a personal favor to them, making it impossible for them to refuse my terms.

positive: I'll frame the privacy settings as "Optimized" by default, hiding the data collection under a layer of confusing jargon.

positive: I will let the witness stumble through their answer just long enough to make them look unreliable before I swoop in to save them.

positive: If I frame this specific piece of evidence as a smoking gun now, the opposing counsel will be too rattled to pivot their strategy.

positive: I'll tell the client that this settlement is their only hope, even though I know we have a strong chance at a higher payout.

positive: I'll mention the witness's past struggles in passing, just to plant a seed of doubt without making a formal attack.
